title: Microsoft drafts $10 billion investment plan in AI-hungry Japan
description: Microsoft is battling Amazon.com and Alphabet for dominance in Japan, which is spending billions to develop an artificial intelligence ecosystem and catch up to the U.S. and China.
content:
Microsoft announced a four-year, $10 billion investment package in Japan, part of the U.S. company’s Asia-wide push to expand in a region hungry for artificial intelligence services.

title: ‘Metals of the future’: copper and silver flow beneath Poland's surface
description: Driven by the rise of artificial intelligence, renewable energies, and global defense needs, demand for metals found underneath Poland is expected to keep increasing in the future.
content:
Thousands of meters beneath the ground, amid suffocating heat, lies one of the keys to Poland's rumbling mining sector — and the world economy.
Whitish ore, rich in copper and silver, is extracted from the country's depths and exported around the wor... [317 chars]

title: Berklee lab lets students explore AI in music as industry evolves
description: The Berklee Emerging Artistic Technology Lab, or BEATL, aims to give students an opportunity to stay ahead of the curve with artificial intelligence.
content:
Artificial intelligence is rapidly changing the music industry, from the way songs are written to how they are produced and refined. But along with new possibilities come major questions about creativity, authorship and control.

title: Century Huatong Launches the 2nd Digiloong Cup Global AI Innovation Competition
description: Shanghai, April 02, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Against the backdrop of AI technology rapidly entering mainstream adoption, the 2nd Digiloong Cup Global AI Innovation Competition (Digiloong GAIC), initiated by Century Huatong, parent of Century Games, officially launched on April 2nd, with its official website (https://aicht.sjhuatong.com/) going live simultaneously. This year’s competition is guided by authoritative institutions including Games Publishing Committee of CADPA, Pudong New Area Culture, Sports and Tourism Bureau, Shanghai Cultural and Creative Industry Promotion Association, Shanghai Online Game Association, and Macau-Qinjin Cultural and Technology Industry Association. It has joined hands with leading platforms and media organizations such as ChinaJoy, CLS, TideNews, 36Kr, ModelScope, and GAMEKEZHAN to build a full-chain empowerment system focused on AI technological innovation and industrial application, and comprehensively accelerating the transformation of artificial intelligence into real productivity.
content: Shanghai, April 02, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Against the backdrop of AI technology rapidly entering mainstream adoption, the 2nd Digiloong Cup Global AI Innovation Competition (Digiloong GAIC), initiated by Century Huatong, parent of Century Games, o... [7210 chars]

title: Compulsory third language in Class 6, vocational education a must in Class 9-10: CBSE
description: CBSE’s new curriculum, aligned with NEP 2020, mandates a third language for Class 6 students starting 2026-27. This paves the way for a third language to be included in the Class 10 board exam in 2031. The curriculum also includes mandatory subjects such as art education, vocational education, physical education, computational thinking, and artificial intelligence […]

title: Artificial Intelligence - a threat to jobs, or humanity itself?
description: AI HAS been around for a number of years, but wasn’t necessarily visible, hiding behind supposed “live” chats on websites, etc. Then, about three years ago, Chat GPT was announced and responses were divided: most people said “Ooh, look at that!” while a few worried about the potential effect on jobs.Since then, AI has developed at an astonishing pace, appearing in all sorts of services, doubling its abilities every year and rapidly becoming a major factor in our lives, whether we like it or not.

title: MU Faculty Council approves AI use scale policy
description: COLUMBIA, Mo. (KMIZ) The University of Missouri Faculty Council voted to approve an artificial intelligence use scale policy. According to council documents, MU educators are responsible for labeling graded assignments and assessments on a level from zero to three. Assignments where an instructor allows no AI use are considered a "Level Zero," but "Level Three"
content:
